question: Looking at the US GDP map, which statement below is the most accurate?

options.A: The GDP differences are impossible to determine from this visualization.
options.B: New York, Pennsylvania, and Arizona all have highly diverse GDP levels.
options.C: New York and Tennessee have similar GDPs.
options.D: The GDP gap between New York and Tennessee might exceed that between Pennsylvania and Arizona.

explanation: New York (432.4$B) and Tennessee (622.6$B) have a 190.2$B GDP difference, while Pennsylvania (89.8$B) and Arizona (90.2$B) have only a 0.4$B difference. However, the categorical color encoding groups New York and Tennessee into the same color bin (350$B+ range), making their large difference appear similar, while Pennsylvania and Arizona appear more different due to different color bins.
